text.title: backup-database-postgres Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the PostgreSQL backup methodology end-to-end.

step.name: scope-and-rpo
step.input: Database list + RPO/RTO target + managed-vs-self-managed flag
step.action: Apply skip-this-methodology gate. If self-managed AND RPO ≤ 24h, continue. If PaaS with no cross-cloud requirement, STOP.
step.decision-gate: If scope == skip, halt and document the managed-backup path instead.

step.name: choose-format
step.action: Pick `-Fc` (custom) for < 50 GB or selective restore; `-Fd -j N` (directory) for > 50 GB and parallel restore; `pg_basebackup`/pgBackRest for PITR.
step.output: backup_format value for the config artefact

step.name: emit-pipeline
step.input: Format choice + secrets source + remote storage URI
step.action: Fill `templates/postgres-backup.sh` skeleton: load secrets from secrets manager, run dump, verify, push to remote, prune local. For PITR, fill `templates/pgbackrest.conf` with stanza + repo + retention.

step.name: wire-monitoring
step.action: Emit a job that scrapes `pg_stat_archiver_failed_count_total` (via postgres_exporter) and alerts on positive rate. Add a daily restore-drill cron pointing at `backup-verification-dr`.
step.decision-gate: If WAL archiving is enabled and monitoring is NOT wired, STOP — a PITR pipeline without WAL monitoring is worse than no PITR.

step.name: validate-and-register
step.action: Run the validator. On exit 0, commit; on exit 1, fix violations and re-run.
step.output: Production pipeline live; first verified backup in S3.
